name: Yakut
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Yakut is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Yakut (Sakha, саха тыла) is a Siberian Turkic language of the Sakha Republic, Russia, written since 1939 in an extended CYRILLIC ALPHABET which adds the letters ҕ ҥ ө һ ү and the digraphs дь нь to the Russian inventory; it has vowel harmony and phonemic long vowels/diphthongs written by doubling. INPUT CONTRACT: Cyrillic Sakha text (Unicode, NFC); earlier Novgorodov and Latin (Yañalif) orthographies are out of scope.
